mnemonics: PACKages Control My Visibility: Public, (default), Class-Private
keyPoints: Packages group related classes and interfaces for better organization and namespace management.
Java provides four access levels: public, protected, (default/package-private), and private.
A class member with no access modifier has package-private visibility, meaning it's accessible only to classes within the same package.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a class must be declared public to be accessible from outside its package.
Assuming protected members are accessible from any subclass, regardless of package (they are, but also to other classes in the same package).
Confusing default (package-private) access with private access, leading to unintended accessibility within the same package.
